Hey guys and welcome to the workbook for module four. Happy made it through the main lesson and now I just want to give a brief recap of those main points and then we're going to move into a few exercises and questions that are really going to support you as you craft out the sequence and write those powerful pre-launch emails that just really reshift and reframe your prospects beliefs. This is really where you're starting to do that heavy lifting of engineering that new reality where your product or your offer or your program makes perfect sense. So just as a recap, the pre-launch sequence will take place right after the confirmation and lead us straight into your cart open or that phase of your launch where your program first becomes available. And this is really that opportunity to cash in on your prospects attention and optimism that you earned in module three and start moving them through that series of reframes and a haas and attitudinal shifts and insights and beliefs that invest them further into that journey that really deepen their belief in that outcome and anchor in their responsibility to act on it. So what we're going to be coaching here through this sequence are a few things. We're going to be coaching complete clarity over why the old way wasn't working for them. And this is what's going to finish that job of relinquishing them of that self-blame and judgment over past failures because now they have full clarity and understanding of it. It's then going to proceed to coach a new set of empowering and action-oriented beliefs and understandings and attitudes and diagnoses. So we're going to be doing this as it relates to the opportunity or outcome they're trying to achieve, the problem past failures and missteps. This is the big point. Why wasn't it their fault? One phrase I see so much in email marketing is it's not your fault, but why wasn't it their fault? You've got to qualify that. You've got to really break it down in order for that to be something they could absorb and believe in and internalize and actually act on. We're going to also be talking about new beliefs around the more empowering way to achieve what they're doing and their abilities and capacities to use that way as well as your ability to lead them and help them. And finally, we're going to create some new beliefs and understandings around competitors or commonly held conventions. One way we do this, one way we really approach this shifting of beliefs and attitudes is by calling out the sacred cows with sour milk. These are the conventional and commonly held but oppressive beliefs that derail the progress of your market. They're the ones most ripe to be let go of. They're the ones that once they see a new way, deliver that biggest aha, because people don't want to hold on to them. It's kind of once they get permission to drop them and see a new, more empowering, optimistic way, they're so ready to do so because these are producing the sour milk. It's kind of like a few examples. I can't build an online business until I learn how to use WordPress, sacred cows, slaughter it. I can't play the guitar until I learn how to read sheet music. Once again, if you want to play stairway to heaven, here are those chords to do it. You don't need to read sheet music and excuse my total lack of guitar knowledge and explaining that. In order to have a six-figure business, I need to have a massive list. What if you don't have a massive list but you're a master at paid traffic and you have an amazing offer that can convert it at a profit? I can't learn how to speak French because I'm terrible at memorizing verbs. I've lived in Canada and Montreal for over 30 years and I still don't know how to speak French. I'm probably one of 2% of Montrealers that don't know how to speak French. I gave up when I was 11 years old and I'm like, I can't memorize these verbs so I might as well quit. But that's probably not the most empowering thing. I wish someone slaughtered that sacred cow for me 20 years ago. I can't meditate because I can't quiet my mind. There might be a new way to go about it and your product might have that mechanism that overcomes that block and allows you to slaughter that sacred cow for your customer. These are the ones you want to really call out. The oppressive ones that derail their progress but yet are so ingrained and hardened and reinforced by the industry, by society, by whatever it is because those are the ones that are going to be the most rewarding for your customer to have released for them and also the most compelling and interesting for them to see, the most engaging for them to see in your emails and in your videos and whatever other mechanism you're using. So I did include also a list of subject lines and sometimes it's fun to work backwards on these. If you're having trouble just brainstorming the sacred cows on their own, sometimes a subject line surprisingly can be a really good leaping off point. So we use a lot of these curiosity driven subject lines that imply a diagnosis and these work particularly well during the pre-lunch. So I'll let you go through these but I'll just kind of walk you through them very quickly as well. So I'll be the first to admit it. So this is you, any kind of confession maybe you used to have that belief in one of those sacred cows and it wasn't serving you. But you may not know about insert that opportunity, keep this between us. So that's you know you're going to fill them in on a secret and it might be something that you don't want to tell everybody right away because it's kind of that new alternative. It's a sacred cow and it might ruffle some feathers. Why blank doesn't work and in that blank it would be one of those sacred cows. Why memorizing verbs doesn't work. For serious that's just fun and quirky. Five reasons you're still experiencing that problem. Once again that's very diagnostic. What you need to know about topic. I can't believe I once believed this, another entry point into that sacred cow. I can't believe I once thought I needed to learn how to code to have an online business and this is serious. This is about six years ago I was on Udemy trying to learn everything I could about Ruby on Rails, HTML, all these coding languages because I thought that was an entry ticket to starting an online business. Can we be honest? You might want to sit down for this. That could be you know that's kind of a starter that's very engaging and implies that you're about to deliver some important information. You don't really believe that do you? I won't be winning any friends. Once again when you're calling out a sacred cow that a lot of people hold dear but your market would be ready to let go you might not be the most popular person in the world. Can I tell you a secret? Why isn't everyone talking about this? This one I swiped this one from somewhere. I remember just kind of immediately grabbed me. Why isn't everyone talking about this? It has that sense of missing out and being left behind as well. That's a really great one to just kind of use as a jumping off point. The real reason X. We see this a lot. The real reason and that's another great jumping off point for talking about sacred cows. Can't believe people still believe this. I wish I learned this sooner. Do you really still believe this? Really in 2017? Three unexpected reasons too. And that too could be the new way of doing things. It could be just approaching your market three unexpected reasons to burn your verb book. You know whatever it is. Why you haven't been able to and the outcome three reasons why. These are just things to experiment. Take any one of these and just use that as a jumping off point if you're having trouble brainstorming your sacred cows. But we're also going to get into another exercise and just a bit to help you out with those. So these are the questions to explore. Once again you've seen these already in the 47 questions workbook. If you did them there great bring them over and revisit them. See if you have anything to add. You probably unpacked some additional insights in the last module. So you will want to give it a second look if you did do it before if you haven't. This is your chance to really invest yourself in these questions and extract the best possible content for this subsection. So question one what about your market struggles breaks your heart and it's that breaking your heart question that really just cuts through so much mentation and into that feeling this of why you do what you do. What reality do you see for them that they don't yet see for themselves. This is that classic frustration you feel when you see a friend or family member or spouse or partner or parent with so much or child with so much potential and so much ability that they don't yet see for themselves and you just kind of want to just like do it for them do it with them. So it's that same thing but with your customer and what pains you most about their inability to see that potential. This is where we're really starting to ramp up that FOMO our own fear of them missing out and what's the biggest excuse your avatar makes for having not achieved a favorable outcome yet. So we're not just countering objections later in the closing sequence. You know we want to know what their objections and their excuses for having not achieved a favorable outcome yet are now and more importantly if we can justify that excuse and validate it that builds a lot of trust especially if we could follow that up with something about you or your method that overcomes that limitation. You know if we could isolate that excuse and validate it for them in the pre-launch once we introduce our method that overcomes that limitation there's this sense of causality and I know I'm using the word loops a lot but there's that causality loop if we could say you haven't achieved it because of X an excuse they're already making and saying yes that's very true that's valid and then later close it and make that excuse non valid because of something in your product that's what creates that loop you know and yeah just kind of informs them that there's no longer a reason to be limited. How do your biggest competitors fall short in overcoming it that's going to be for your positioning. What's a false belief your prospect has about the industry about themselves or their ability to achieve it maybe about the best way to solve the problem or achieve the outcome and why might it be a good thing they've waited until now this is always one of my favorite ones to play around with and then we're going to get to those sacred cows this is where you want to identify them these are going to be kind of that crux of your pre-launch sequence if you're doing videos you're going to address either one or more of these in each video if you're doing email only like we're going to show you later sometimes I'll dedicate one email to each one of these beliefs you know coaching a new belief coaching release of a sacred cow in each email so it doesn't have to go in this order you can play around with them but generally speaking as a starting point I like to have three of them I like one that relates to the outcome on a macro level example it's impossible to make a living as a yoga teacher you need a big email list to have a successful online business so we're really starting macro then we get a little more a little a little more zoomed in you know to the obstacle and problem you know writing copy is a hard and painful process you know a lot of people believe that and for a lot of people it's true it is but does it have to be that's something we can question I'm a yoga teacher I shouldn't have to sell I just want to serve shouldn't I just trust that the people I meant to serve will find me so that's about their experience in trying to become a well compensated yoga teacher and then we get even more micro and granular to their abilities you know to achieve that outcome so I'm terrible at the techie stuff there's no way I can manage half a dozen social media accounts and all the other business things I need to do can someone just do that for me so that could be a sacred cow for someone who hasn't really been initiated to online business sees people doing all these things and thinks they need to do it all and that's not something they're good at or want to do either so those sacred cows are going to really be the crux of it and then you want to follow that up with the reversals or reframes or a has that counter them so to continue on that example number one would be you're right you can't make a living as a yoga teacher but you can make an amazing living and have a great lifestyle as a yoga entrepreneur would you be open to shifting your definition so that's more of a paradigm level shift you know a view shift how exactly are you being of service when you're stressing out about how you're going to cover next month's rent would it be possible that true service doesn't require endless self-sacrifice but a more harmonious and synergistic relationship with money and the business side so that's how we would counter that I don't want to sell spiel that a lot of yoga teachers and just people in that industry feel around it and then the third ah in this case would be while being on every single social media channel maybe needed for a yoga celebrity it's by no means necessary to have a thriving career as a yoga entrepreneur so slaughtering that sacred cow much like your practice running a successful business doesn't rely on a hundred different poses but precision mindfulness and consistency on the few that truly serve you and help you grow so just kind of using a familiar metaphor for them to really drive in that new aha and insight so you slaughter that sacred cow and you replace it immediately with that reversal that reframe that aha that empowers them and invigorates them and energizes them to continue on that journey and just adds to this new layer of believability whenever we see things in a new way we also simultaneously let go of some doubt you know when we let go of baggage we kind of can rise a little more and just kind of get more perspective and more clarity and more encouragement of what's to come so that's it I want you to go through that exercise I want you to jot down at least three sacred cows and the reframe the associated reframe that is really going to fuel this journey and give them that spark and give them that renewed hope optimism excitement and in this case by the end of the prelaunch total believability about the outcome to come so that's it we've got two theme place to support this you're going to need to have completed this exercise before we get into them but once you've done that meet us back for theme plate number six which is going to be slaughter house five which is going to be the one you're going to want to look at if you're doing that minimum viable funnel and then we have the plate number seven which is going to be slaughter house four so a smaller variation if you're going to be pointing your prospect to a video or something else that delivers the reframe so that's it for here I will see you in the next video
